content: 2024-2025 methanol poisoning outbreaks in Southeast Asia, Africa, and Eastern Europe highlight ongoing public health challenges. WHO reported 2,800+ deaths globally in 2024, primarily from contaminated illicit alcohol. New point-of-care testing devices using enzymatic assays can now detect methanol in beverages within 15 minutes, enabling rapid field screening. Treatment protocols have evolved to include earlier hemodialysis initiation (methanol level >50 mg/dL or pH <7.25) and high-dose folate regimens. The 2025 International Consensus on Methanol Management recommends standardized fomepizole dosing and expanded access programs for low-resource settings.
source: WHO Global Methanol Surveillance Report 2025 & Clinical Toxicology
